data.title: The Trade Desk Crashes: Here's The Price I'll Buy (Again!).
data.description:
What happened to The Trade Desk stock?

Wow! Just when it looks like high-flying stocks will continue defying reasonable valuations, the market delivers a bruising lesson. The Trade Desk released what the market deemed disappointing results. Revenue came in at $741 million, $19 million below expectations. Before Q4, The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) hadn't posted an earnings miss in 33 straight quarters.

The results weren't awful, but it wasn't good enough when a stock trades for more than 20 times sales. As you can see below, the stock plummeted.

It is nearly 50% off its recent highs. I've been bullish about The Trade Desk, especially in 2022 and early 2023, when I built a position from $42 to $68 per share. The majority of my shares were called away after selling $105 calls, sold more at $130, and have had just a token position since.

It's looking like time to get back in soon.

How bad were Q4 earnings?

It's not that Q4 was abysmal, and the full year of 2024 was quite good. As shown below, sales grew 26% for the year, an increase over the 23% in 2023.

However, guidance for Q1 2025 is for just 17% growth - a major backward step.

Founder and CEO Jeff Green was contrite on the earnings call:

While we’re proud of these milestones, I want to acknowledge up front that for the first time in 33 quarters as a public company, we fell short of our own expectations. During COVID, we revised our expectations once along with the rest of the markets, but for the first time in 8 years, we missed the expectations we set, and it was our fault.

....we did the largest reorganization in company history in December. While we often make structural changes at the end of the year to improve our business, this was bigger than usual.

This sounds ominous. Green went on to lay out a four-stage plan of action.

  1. The company clarified employees' roles and streamlined sales teams.
  2. Renewed focus on internal effectiveness and scalability.
  3. Dedicated more resources to brand relationships and joint business plans (this part of the business is growing much quicker than other areas).
  4. Shrunk product development teams to become more nimble.

Reading between the lines: It sounds like the company became stagnant, had too many cooks in the kitchen, the salesforce wasn't performing as well as expected, and new products were too slow to come to the market.

This isn't good news; however, recognizing the problems and formulating a detailed action plan is critical. And that is what Green did. COVID-19 forced Airbnb to do something similar in 2020, and the results have been terrific. Here are Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky's comments on the shift:

…on headcount, something that's really interesting happened. So obviously, in 2020, we had to make some really difficult decisions, and we became a much smaller and more focused company. And the obvious result of that is that we got more efficient and more profitable. But there was a less obvious result. What ended up happening is we had fewer people in meetings, and people can move a lot faster. And we concentrate all of our very best people and put them on only a few problems.

The streamlining was very successful. Jeff Green is a terrific CEO, so my confidence is high that the changes will work well over the long haul.

Is The Trade Desk a buy now?

The Trade Desk is well positioned in a growing industry. Programmatic advertising and streaming television will continue to increase. Advertisers love data, feedback, and targeted placement, which is The Trade Desk's wheelhouse. According to Perplexity, the average American home subscribes to 4.5 streaming services in 2025, up from 3.9 last year.

Live sports is a tailwind for streaming as well. The latest development is Netflix (NFLX) reportedly preparing a bid for a full slate of Sunday NFL football game rights. The exodus of sports from cable will continue since the major networks don't have as deep of pockets as the Netflix and Amazons (AMZN) of the world.

The company's finances are in excellent shape. Cash and investments increased from $1.4 billion as of Q4 2023 to $1.9 billion at the end of 2024. Receivables also increased from $2.9 billion to $3.3 billion, and the company is long-term debt-free.

GAAP operating income for 2024 increased to $430 million in 2024 compared to $200 million the prior year. The operating margin grew to 17% from 10%, proving the company has operating leverage.

Finally (finally!), The Trade Desk's stock valuation is attractive again. We are nearly back to the low levels at the beginning of 2023.

On sales:

And on cash flow:

The stock's valuation has nearly retraced all the gains over the last two years.

I am bullish on The Trade Desk and have been waiting for an opportunity to buy shares again. This looks like a good time to begin rebuilding a position. The changes will take time to succeed, so the stock may drift for a while. However, the company released Q1 guidance on February 12th, nearly halfway through the quarter. You can bet they are confident they will beat those numbers.

data.title: S&P 500: It's Happening Again. Week Starting 24th February (Technical Analysis)
data.description:
It was another red Friday for the S&P 500 (SPY), the fifth in a row. It makes you wonder what news could come out over the weekend and the large Monday drops on 27th January (Deep Seek) and the 3rd February (the trade war) seem to have made investors risk averse into the end of the week.

Last weekend's article pointed out the bullish technicals and the potential for a break-out. This looked to be playing out as Wednesday's session closed at a new all-time high of 6144, but things quickly soured and the break of 6100 warned all was not right (see last week's final sentence).

This week's article looks at why Friday's drop could be another sharp but short-lived move and explores how something more bearish could develop.

S&P 500 Monthly

There is only one week to go until February's close and it is fulfilling the stat that February is traditionally one of the weaker months of the year. Last week's rally made a new all-time high, but by Friday the S&P500 was already trading below January's close. A potentially bearish bar (a doji) would form with a close near or below the February open of 5969.

The S&P500 has struggled to break the measured move and Fibonacci extension highlighted in these articles since September. 6118 is where the bull market starting at the 2022 low is equal in length to the 2020-2022 bull market 6145 is the 200% extension of the 2022 bear market.

Support on the monthly chart is obvious at the January 5773 low, but it is also worth noting the December close of 5881 where the 2025 annual bar would shift from green to red. 5669-74 at the July high and the October bottom is a major support level below that.

An upside DeMARK exhaustion count is on bar 5 (of 9) in February. This means there can be no signal until at least May.

S&P 500 Weekly

This week's bar was bearish with a failed new high and weak close at the lows. It's the kind of action that can lead to tops/large corrections and if the monthly chart also develops a reversal, we should be extra cautious. Note that none of the previous five weeks had a bearish pattern which made it easier to buy the dips on DeepSeek, tariffs, CPI, etc...

Resistance is at 6100 and 6147. The red channel high will be above 6300 next week, so it is too distant to be relevant.

Initial support is the weekly low of 6003. The weekly 20sma is worth noting around 5960 as it caught several bottoms, but it has also been cut through several times so it should be in confluence with other factors to be trustworthy.

An upside DeMARK exhaustion count was aborted this week by the drop. Failure to reach completion is a feature of choppy markets. A new downside count will be on bar 2 (of 9) next week.

S&P 500 Daily

Wednesday's session could only be described as quietly bullish. Thursday's session was a warning but could still be regarded as neutral. Friday's drop was therefore slightly surprising, although by now we should all be on guard going into the weekend. Good supports were broken and the weak close at the 50sma suggests there will be follow-through next week.

Initial resistance is around 6050 at the high volume area, then 6084-100.

6003-6008 is initial support at the previous low and the 50sma but looks vulnerable. 5962 is a previous reversal point and lines up with the 20sma. Below that, the 5923 low and the blue channel at 5945 are major support.

A downside DeMARK exhaustion count will be on bar 2 (of 9) on Monday. That means no signal can be completed next week. Indeed, no signal has been completed since 4th December as trends keep reversing.

Drivers/Events

Data was on the quiet side last week and the sell-off didn't have one principal driver; rather it seemed a combination of recent red flags. Market moves on Friday support the view that the fears revolve around a slower economy. Oil dropped sharply and bonds were higher. Walmart (WMT) earnings and lower-than-expected consumer sentiment were just the cherry on the top.

Recent failed break-outs underline the view that Mag 7 stocks must participate for any sustained rally to unfold. Nvidia's (NVDA) earnings next Wednesday may be the deciding factor on whether this important group of stocks will lead again or continue to stagnate. Guidance will be watched closely in light of DeepSeek.

Prelim GDP and unemployment claims will be released on Thursday. The data highlight of the week is on Friday with the release of the PCE Index. This is expected to rise to 0.3%, which is too high but not a level to cause panic. A few lower prints in the following months combined with weaker data would still open the door for Fed cuts in May/June.

One last thing to monitor is USDJPY. With yields rising in Japan and falling in the US, is the carry trade unwinding and putting additional pressure on stocks?

Probable Moves Next Week(s)

The S&P500 has done it again: another failed break-out and another Friday plunge. This time it has created a weekly reversal and the monthly chart could also develop a reversal pattern if the February bar closes below 5969 this coming Friday. It makes buying the dip riskier than in previous weeks when the technicals were still clearly bullish, but there is not enough evidence yet to say this is different than other sharp drops in recent months.

Friday's action suggests there will be more downside on Monday or Tuesday. This could come immediately after some negative news out over the weekend, or following a brief bounce if bad news fails to materialize. 6050 may act as initial resistance. A break of 6000 should target 5945-62 where this leg down could end and lead to another full recovery to 6100 and even new highs.

Bigger picture, the sluggish action over the last 3 months should eventually lead to a larger correction to reset positioning and sentiment. 5669 is a reasonable initial target in this phase. Closing next week below 5969 would suggest it is already underway.

data.title: Ariel Investments Emerging Markets Value Ex-China Q4 2024 Commentary
data.description:
Markets worldwide defied expectations in 2024 led by the dominating performance of U.S. mega-cap technology companies and investor enthusiasm for artificial intelligence (AI) themed stocks. However, concerns surrounding the new U.S. administration’s policies, a strengthening U.S. dollar, slower economic growth in India, political turmoil in Korea and the prospect of wider fiscal deficits in Brazil drove the MSCI Emerging Markets ex-China Index into the red. Although uncertainty and volatility are likely to remain elevated, the patient investor knows “stock prices trade on fundamentals. And when those solid fundamentals shine through, share prices rise. 1” Against this backdrop, the Ariel Emerging Markets Value ex-China Composite decreased - 8.56% gross of fees (-8.80% net of fees) in the quarter, falling short of the -8.12% return of the MSCI EM ex-China Index. Over the trailing one-year period, the Ariel Emerging Markets Value ex-China Composite advanced +4.36% gross of fees (+3.23% net of fees), outpacing the +3.56% return posted by the MSCI EM ex-China Index.

Emaar Properties PJSC, a property investment and development company in the MENA region was the top contributor over the period on strong earnings results, highlighted by record quarterly residential sales, healthy margins and robust free cash flow. The company also announced an attractive long-term dividend policy to return capital to shareholders. Looking ahead, we believe Emaar Properties will continue to benefit from the post COVID recovery in Dubai’s real estate market, which has been amplified by a large number of expatriates entering the country.

Taiwanese producer of electronic connectors Lotes Co., Ltd., also advanced in the quarter as robust consumer demand for new generations of CPU sockets continues to drive earnings momentum. The company’s improved product mix resulted in strong pricing and margin expansion. Management also announced Lotes would enter the AI data center connector market which boosted its shares. Looking ahead, we think the CPU socket market will continue to grow, as new generations of these electrical components require more complex and expensive sockets. We believe Lotes will benefit from future rollouts given its leading market position and strong historical relationship with CPU designers Intel and AMD. Lastly, we continue to appreciate the company’s commitment to return capital to shareholders.

Additionally, Mexico-based cement producer, GCC SAB de CV traded higher during the quarter. Although volumes continue to reflect a challenging economic environment, favorable pricing and solid cost containment drove gross margin expansion and a bottom-line beat. Meanwhile, the U.S. election outcome further aided share price appreciation on the potential for lower U.S. corporate taxes and greater construction activity. In our view, GCC continues to trade at a meaningful valuation discount to its U.S. building material peers given its pricing power in a tight cement market, robust balance sheet and strong cash flow generation.

Conversely, banking services provider, IndusInd Bank Ltd. headquartered in India, which specializes in microfinance as well as personal and commercial vehicle loans, traded lower in the quarter. The company’s quarterly earnings results deteriorated on weaker asset quality in the microfinance segment. However, this segment makes up a small portion of its total loan book. We expect to see improvements over the next few quarters given the short duration of the loans and the company’s reduction in new lending. With shares trading just above book value despite the company’s strong growth potential, solid capitalization ratios and expectations for healthy mid-cycle profitability, we see attractive upside.

Leading Korean producer of camera modules for smartphones, LG Innotek Company Ltd. also underperformed following mixed quarterly earnings results. While revenues came in better-than-expected, margins were meaningfully lower due to FX headwinds and intensifying competition in key customer camera module markets. Meanwhile, the company’s primary customer, Apple, saw muted initial sales of its newest-generation iPhone 16 model further weighing on shares.

Despite these developments, we remain enthusiastic LG Innotek will continue to benefit from a combination of growing camera content in new smartphones, as well as a pickup in high-end smartphone sales as consumers replace their existing phones. In addition, free cash flow generation should increase as the company’s capital expenditures decline following ample investments in recent years. LG Innotek’s deep valuation discount versus comparable companies adds to our excitement about the upside potential for the stock.

Lastly, leading Brazilian health insurance company, Hapvida Participacoes e Investimentos SA, declined over the period. The increasing trend of lawsuits against healthcare providers in Brazil weighed on shares, as much higher expenses for provisioning hurt profitability. Meanwhile, a weakening macroeconomic outlook for Brazil, underscored by worries around the government’s fiscal policies also sparked investor concerns. As a result, we exited the name to pursue more compelling opportunities.

We initiated five new positions in the quarter.

We purchased Bank of Georgia Group PLC which offers banking and financial services. The company presents an attractive investment opportunity given its robust profitability and strong loan growth outlook. Meanwhile, Georgia’s economy is benefitting from growing tourism and exports, as well as from foreign direct investment. The firm’s recent accretive acquisition of Ameriabank in Armenia should further support asset and earnings growth over time.

We added Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur Tbk PT (OTCPK:PNDFF)(OTCPK:PIFFY), which is the leading packaged foods producer in Indonesia with a growing market presence in the Middle East, Africa and parts of Eastern Europe. Indofood’s product categories include noodles, dairy, snacks, seasonings, nutrition and special foods, beverages and packaging. The company enjoys high margins due to its strong brands and superior distribution network.

Looking ahead, we expect supportive government policies, an improving economic outlook for low-income segments of the population and the company’s rapid growth in international markets will serve as tailwinds for robust future earnings growth.

We bought Peruvian bank specializing in retail loans, Intercorp Financial Services Inc. (IFS). We expect a recovery in asset quality driven by an improving macroeconomic backdrop as well as the company’s own self- help efforts to clean its books following a challenging period over the prior two years. Despite an operating environment that supports robust cyclical profitability over time, the stock is currently trading at a substantial discount to our internal estimates.

We purchased Brazilian apparel company, Lojas Renner (OTCPK:LRENY) which designs, develops and sells apparel, footwear and accessories. We believe the company is poised for higher earnings growth given recent investments to capture cost efficiencies and improve its competitive positioning. Notably, we expect the ramp up of its new distribution center to drive greater store productivity, lower markdowns via better inventory management, reduced logistics costs and higher margins. Our favorable outlook is further underpinned by an improving credit book and a less competitive operating environment given recent changes to import taxes.

Finally, we re-initiated a position in leading pure-play producer of memory semiconductor products, SK Hynix Inc. (OTCPK:HXSCF) Shares traded meaningfully lower following our exit from the name earlier in the year, presenting us with an attractive re-entry point. In our view, SK Hynix is well-positioned to benefit from increased deployment of its leading-edge High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) products, which are experiencing rapid demand growth to power Artificial Intelligence (AI).

Meanwhile, we exited leading Brazilian health insurance company, Hapvida Participacoes e Investimentos SA; Korean-based leading manufacturer of mobile phones and smartphones, Samsung Electronics Company, Ltd. (OTCPK:SSNLF); Brazilian food retailer, Sendas Distribuidora SA (OTC:ASAIY); and global semiconductor foundry, United Microelectronics Corp. (UMC) to pursue more compelling opportunities.

Uncertainty surrounding the potential impact of the new U.S. administration’s policies on Emerging Market equities presents an attractive entry point to one of the most mispriced asset classes in the world. We believe concerns over stagnation in developing economies due to U.S. tariffs are overstated. We see the path to replace imports with U.S. production given the country’s unemployment rate of 4.1% and its citizens’ strong propensity to consume particularly challenging. In our view, a significant reduction in U.S. imports would require a large economic slowdown or materially higher U.S. savings rate. Notably, EM’s valuation discount relative to the developed markets remains wide and does not reflect our expectation that corporate earnings growth in developing economies will exceed the earnings growth outlook in advanced economies over the near-to-medium term. Rising productivity, a burgeoning middle class and growing urbanization should drive local demand and superior economic growth. Additionally, many EM companies across markets are becoming more diligent in their capital allocation policies.

They are focusing on investments to capture efficiencies and increase returns as well as returning capital to shareholders through dividends and buybacks. Meanwhile, the U.S. Dollar is nearing a 20-year high despite an increasingly challenged U.S. fiscal position. Hence, we think there could be upside from a weaker U.S. Dollar over the medium term. Against this backdrop, we strongly believe our emerging market value portfolios are well-positioned to deliver superior returns over the long run.

Footnote

1 Hobson, Mellody and John W. Rogers Jr. “What the Stock market Taught Us This Year: Don’t Fall for These Investing Traps.” Wall Street Journal, 5 December 2023.

Investments in non-U.S. securities may underperform and may be more volatile than comparable U.S. stocks because of the risks involving non-U.S. economies, markets, political systems, regulatory standards, currencies and taxes. The use of currency derivatives and ETFs may increase investment losses and expenses and create more volatility. Investments in emerging markets present additional risks, such as difficulties in selling on a timely basis and at an acceptable price. The intrinsic value of the stocks within the strategy may never be recognized by the broader market. The strategy is often concentrated in fewer sectors than its benchmarks, and its performance may suffer if these sectors underperform the overall stock market.

Past performance does not guarantee future results. For the period ended 12/31/2024, the performance (net of fees) of the Ariel Emerging Markets Value ex-China Composite for the 1- year and since inception on 5/31/2023 was +3.23% and +12.82%, respectively. For the period ended 12/31/2024, the performance for the MSCI EM ex-China Net Index over the 1- year and since inception of the Ariel Emerging Markets Value ex-China Composite on 5/31/2023 was +3.56% and +10.61%, respectively. Ariel Composite Net of Fees returns are calculated by deducting the actual monthly advisory fee (on an asset-weighted basis) applicable to all accounts in the composite, using the fee rates in place as of the most recent calendar quarter-end. Gross returns do not reflect the deduction of advisory fees. Client returns will be reduced by advisory fees and such other expenses as may be incurred in the management of the account. Advisory fees are described in Part 2 of Ariel’s Form ADV. Returns assume the reinvestment of dividends and other earnings. Returns are expressed in U.S. dollars. Current performance may be lower or higher than the performance data quoted. The Ariel Emerging Markets Value ex-China Composite differs from its benchmark, the MSCI EM ex-China Net Index, because the Composite has fewer holdings than the benchmark.

The opinions expressed are current as of the date of this commentary but are subject to change. The information provided in this commentary does not provide information reasonably sufficient upon which to base an investment decision and should not be considered a recommendation to purchase or sell any particular security. There is no guarantee that any of the views expressed will come to fruition or any investment will perform as described.

As of 12/31/2024, Emaar Properties PJSC constituted 2.4% of the Ariel Emerging Markets Value ex-China Composite (representative portfolio); Lotes Company, Ltd. 1.9%; GCC SAB de CV 1.8%; IndusInd Bank Limited 1.7%; LG Innotek Company, Ltd. 1.7%; Hapvida Participacoes e Investimentos SA 0.0%; Bank of Georgia Group PLC 0.7%; Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur Tbk PT 0.8%; Intercorp Financial Services Inc. 1.2%; Lojas Renner SA 1.4%; SK Hynix Inc. 3.6%; Samsung Electronics Co Ltd. 0.0%; Sendas Distribuidora S/A 0.0% and United Microelectronics Corporation 0.0%.

Portfolio holdings are subject to change. The performance of any single portfolio holding is no indication of the performance of other portfolio holdings of the Ariel Emerging Markets Value ex-China Composite.

A glossary of financial terms provided herein may be found on our website at www.arielinvestments.com.

Indexes are unmanaged. Investors cannot invest directly in an index. MSCI Emerging Markets ex-China Index captures large and mid cap representation across 23 of the 24 Emerging Markets (EM) countries excluding China. Its inception date is March 9, 2017. All MSCI Index net returns reflect the reinvestment of income and other earnings, including the dividends net of the maximum withholding tax applicable to non-resident institutional investors that do not benefit from double taxation treaties. MSCI uses the maximum tax rate applicable to institutional investors, as determined by the companies’ country of incorporation. MSCI makes no express or implied warranties or representations and shall have no liability whatsoever with respect to any MSCI data contained herein. The MSCI data may not be further redistributed or used to create indices or financial products. This report is not approved or produced by MSCI.

data.title: Ariel Investments Ariel Global Fund Q4 2024 Commentary
data.description:
Markets worldwide defied expectations in 2024 led by the dominating performance of U.S. mega-cap technology companies and investor enthusiasm for artificial intelligence (AI) themed stocks. However, persistent challenges in the Eurozone, including uncertainty around interest rates, concerns surrounding the new U.S. administration’s policies as well as a strengthening U.S. dollar drove the MSCI ACWI, MSCI ACWI ex-U.S. and MSCI EAFE Indices into the red. China’s economy also ended the year sluggishly, with September’s stimulus efforts losing steam and the recovery in consumer spending turning elusive. Although uncertainty and volatility are likely to remain elevated, the patient investor knows “stock prices trade on fundamentals. And when those solid fundamentals shine through, share prices rise. 1” Against this backdrop, the Ariel Global Composite fell -3.51% gross of fees (-3.65% net of fees) in the quarter, trailing the -0.99% return posted by the MSCI ACWI Index, but ahead of the -4.71% decline of the MSCI ACWI Value Index. Over the last one-year period, the Ariel Global Composite advanced +8.12% gross of fees (+7.55% net of fees), underperforming the MSCI ACWI Index’s +17.49% gain and the +10.76% return of the MSCI ACWI Value Index.

Ariel’s non-consensus approach seeks to identify undervalued, out-of-favor, franchises that are misunderstood and therefore mispriced. The Ariel Global bottom-up strategy is overweight Consumer Discretionary, Financials, Health Care and Utilities, meaningfully underweight Industrials, Consumer Staples, Information Technology and Communication Services, as well as lacks exposure to Energy and Materials. At the sector level, our allocation to currency forwards, Real Estate holdings and avoidance of Materials were the greatest contributors to returns, while our investment choices within Consumer Discretionary, Information Technology and Communication Service were the largest performance detractors.

Emaar Properties PJSC, a property investment and development company in the MENA region, traded higher over the period on strong earnings results, highlighted by record quarterly residential sales, healthy margins and robust free cash flow. Additionally, the company announced an attractive long-term dividend policy to return capital to shareholders. Looking ahead, we believe Emaar Properties

will continue to benefit from the post COVID recovery in Dubai’s real estate market, which has been amplified by a large number of expatriates entering the country.

Global financial services company, Capital One Financial Corporation (COF) was another top performer during the period. Shares rallied following the U.S. election as investors believe the potential Discover Financial Services (DFS) acquisition is more likely to close. The new administration is expected to be more accommodative of bank mergers and acquisitions. In our view, the deal would produce significant long-term earnings accretion. COF will be able to leverage DFS’ proprietary payments network, enabling direct interaction with merchants and consumers. This closed loop dynamic should lead to higher volumes of credit card conversions boosting its shares. At current levels, we view the long-term outlook to be attractive, given favorable business trends, stabilizing delinquency rates within the credit card industry, upside from the DFS acquisition and COF’s enhanced focus on technology.

New holding, Italian bank, Banca Monte dei Paschi (OTCPK:BMDPF) also advanced following a quarterly earnings beat and subsequent raise in full year guidance. The Italian government also sold a portion of its stake in the firm further aiding share price appreciation. We believe the bank can provide long-term earnings stability through its recent actions to reduce interest rate sensitivity and improve asset quality, thereby enhancing profitability and the balance sheet. Additionally, the bank's increased payout ratio to 75% and attractive cash yield—the highest among European Union banks—bolsters our positive outlook. Furthermore, we think a favorable interest rate outlook alongside the company’s solid capital profile and robust trading book as well as the potential for consolidation in the Italian banking sector presents additional upside potential.

Alternatively, several positions weighed on performance. French multinational tire manufacturer, Michelin (CDGE) was a notable underperformer in the period due to a challenging macro environment. Weaker-than-expected consumer demand caused a sharp decline in volumes resulting in to a top- and bottom-line earnings miss. Subsequently, management cut 2024 guidance and announced plans to close two of its factories by 2026 to reduce costs. Nonetheless, we remain enthusiastic about Michelin's strong global competitive position and pricing strategy. In our view, shares are trading at a large discount to historical multiples despite a stronger balance sheet, higher free cash flow generation and attractive returns of capital to shareholders.

China-based E-commerce company, JD.com, Inc. (JD)(OTCPK:JDCMF) also detracted from performance in the period. The stock came under pressure as some investors took profits on solid earnings performance, while others became concerned with the implications tariffs could have on the Chinese economy. In our view, this share price action runs counter to the company's solid business fundamentals. The home appliance trade-in program and popular shopping event, Singles' Day, generated significant consumer spending across various product categories. Additionally, the company’s strategic decision to diversify general merchandise product offerings, expand its third-party marketplace business and monetize advertising streams continues to aid the top- and bottom-lines. Despite the near-term noise, we continue to view the company’s strategic positioning favorably and like JD.com’s long-term growth prospects.

Lastly, American healthcare company, CVS Health Corporation (CVS) underperformed in the period. The company preannounced a third-quarter preliminary profit estimate materially below consensus expectations and pulled its 2024 guidance due to continued medical cost pressures. Investor concerns around the recently proposed Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) legislation further weighed on shares. Despite these challenges, management reiterated its focus on improving margins and enhancing its positioning in Medicare Advantage (MA). CVS believes the program can remain an attractive business for Aetna and CVS Health over time as it implements a multi-year repricing strategy across plan level benefits. Meanwhile, CVS continues to take actions to drive long-term success including the appointment of longtime company executive David Joyner as President and CEO as well as adding four new board members.

In addition to purchasing Italian bank, Banca Monte dei Paschi, we initiated eight new positions in the quarter.

We added research-based biopharmaceutical company, AbbVie, Inc. (ABBV) following a recent pullback in shares related to a Phase 2 clinical failure for schizophrenia drug, Emraclidine. Despite the setback, we believe AbbVie’s core inflammation and immunology (I&I) business is set to outperform. We expect next-generation I&I drugs, Skyrizi and Rinvoq, used in the treatment of inflammatory bowel disease, to be key long-term revenue drivers.

We bought global bank and financial services provider, Barclays PLC (BCS)(OTCPK:BCLYF). We expect shares to benefit from a recovery in global capital markets and net interest income (NII) growth driven by macroeconomic hedging and asset flows. The bank is also planning to expand its investment banking advisory business. Moreover, its U.S. credit card business presents opportunities for either a potential sale or a quicker earnings recovery. Taken together, we see a reasonable path for Barclays to pursue its return targets, which include the distribution of £3 billion and £10 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases between 2024 and 2026 and achieving a return on tangible common equity of about 12%.

We initiated a position in specialty insurance company, Beazley plc (OTCPK:BZLYF)(OTCPK:BZLEY). The firm’s diversified portfolio offerings include commercial property, specialty risk and cyber insurance. We find the growth and profitability profile of its property segment to be attractive as 100% of its business in North America is being generated by the booming Excess and Surplus (E&S) market—an area that caters to non-traditional, high-risk insurance needs. Cyber also presents an exciting structural growth opportunity for the company, particularly since insurance penetration of information assets is low.

Looking ahead, we find Beazley’s earnings outlook to be attractive, supported by its robust business mix, solid margins and improving capital profile.

We purchased CenterPoint Energy, Inc. (CNP) which is a utility company that owns and operates electric and natural gas transmission and distribution facilities primarily in Texas.

Shares came under pressure following political pushback on CNP’s poor response and restoration efforts following Hurricane Beryl. The uncertainty ignited concerns around the company’s balance sheet health and regulatory risks. We view the sell off as an overreaction and believe CNP is at an inflection point where recent infrastructure upgrades and momentum around a potential divestiture of its mobile generation assets to another entity should improve CNP’s regulatory standing. We think the company has a strong growth profile underpinned by economic expansion in Texas and see this supporting long-term earnings growth.

Furthermore, management’s execution of asset sales in 2025 should further stabilize the company’s balance sheet.

We added leading European telecommunications services operator, Orange SA (OTC:ORANY). The company offers mobile, fixed-line and broadband services to roughly 300 million customers across 26 countries. Orange SA has a strong market presence in France and Spain and is expanding operations across the Middle East and Africa (MEA). We believe positive subscriber trends and rational price increases across these markets will support long term revenue growth. For instance, Orange is gaining market share on its network and service quality in France; benefitting from merger synergies and monetizing the fiber business in Spain; and offering mobile data services to significantly under-penetrated regions in MEA. Meanwhile, as the company completes its fiber-roll-out in France, we expect higher profitability, free cash flow generation and increasing shareholder returns.

We purchased Redeia Corp SA (OTCPK:RDEIF)(OTCPK:RDEIY), a Spain-based electricity provider. We believe Redeia’s ability to generate robust earnings growth with further upside from additional regulatory drivers is currently being underappreciated by the market.

We also bought shares of leading pure-play producer of memory semiconductor products, SK Hynix Inc. (OTCPK:HXSCF) In our view, SK Hynix is well positioned to benefit from increased deployment of its leading-edge High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) products, which are experiencing rapid demand growth to power Artificial Intelligence (AI).

Finally, we initiated a position in Sega Sammy Holdings Inc. (OTCPK:SGAMY)(OTCPK:SGAMF), a Japanese holding company specializing in the development of video games and the production of pachinko and pachislot machines. We believe the company is poised to deliver higher earnings, driven by robust growth within its video game segment. This is largely due to the company’s new multiplatform storytelling strategy centered around the firm’s core intellectual property: Sonic, Atlus, and Like a Dragon.

We believe it will help drive sales of both old and new titles, thereby boosting margins. Meanwhile, the pachinko and pachislot segment remains a profitable and stable cash flow generator for the company.

By comparison, we successfully exited Brazilian insurance company, BB Seguridade Participacoes SA (OTCPK:BBSEY), financial holding company, Comerica, Inc. (CMA), diagnostics leader, Roche Holding AG (OTCQX:RHHBY)(OTCQX:RHHBF)(OTCPK:RHHVF) and leading mobile telecommunications company, Telefónica Brasil SA (VIV) on valuation. We also sold our position in biopharmaceutical company, Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMY), global pharmaceutical and healthcare company, GSK plc (GSK) and Italian gas distributor, Italgas Spa (OTCPK:ITGGF) to pursue more compelling opportunities.

The stock market dynamics of 2024 highlight a transformative shift in the global economy, fueled by advancements in artificial intelligence and geopolitical fragmentation. While the narrow ascent of AI themed stocks have driven market returns in recent years, opportunities should broaden to companies utilizing AI to create real-world efficiencies. As we head into 2025, there may be an over-reliance on the U.S. stock market to drive returns. With uncertainty surrounding rate cuts as well as the policy agenda for the new US administration, we expect heightened market volatility. We see this environment as conducive for active managers, whose knowledge, expertise and forward thinking enables them to look beyond the short-term noise and identify opportunities.

Accordingly, we are finding many mispriced global and international stocks trading at a discount to their U.S. counterparts. Their valuation is attractive, profitability less vulnerable and balance sheets remain strong. Against this backdrop, our international and global portfolios are well- positioned to deliver stronger returns over the long run.

Footnote

1 Hobson, Mellody and John W. Rogers Jr. “What the Stock market Taught Us This Year: Don’t Fall for These Investing Traps.” Wall Street Journal, 5 December 2023.

data.title: Undercovered Dozen: Hammond Power Solutions, Blaize Holdings, Xiaomi, Coherent +
data.description:
Undercovered Dozen Introduction

The 'Undercovered' Dozen is a weekly Seeking Alpha editor-curated series highlighting 12 articles on lesser covered stocks from the previous seven days. We hope this series provides ideas and inspires discussion.

Today, we're looking at articles published between February 14th - 20th.

Take a look at what these less-covered stock ideas might hold for you. And please join the conversation below: are any of these worth following up on? And are there other undercovered ideas that you like?

Woodside Energy: Assets Keep Performing, Time To Get In

Maxime Emile | Buy

This company has a $29 billion market cap, an 8.38% yield, and an EBITDA margin of 63% yet a stock price that is down over 30% in the last 12 months. Shares of Woodside Energy Group Ltd (NYSE:WDS) have seen nothing but neglect from the market, it seems. With such a beaten-down share price, is it time to finally get into the stock and open a position?

In December, WDS made a deal with Chevron Corporation (CVX) to simplify the portfolio in an asset swap along with a $400 million payment from CVX as well. Moves like this will over the long term solidify a higher margin for WDS in my opinion. In short, the company's assets are still performing very well, and WDS investing aggressively in new gas and oil projects is not a step in the wrong direction. Market pessimism has opened up a fantastic buying opportunity instead.

WDS is included in the Energy sector and more specifically the Oil and Gas Exploration and Production industry. Its assets are located all over the world, but what sets it apart somewhat from the most commonly covered oil and gas companies here on Seeking Alpha is that WDS has a decent footprint in Australia, which opens up the Asian energy market. In July 2024, WDS made a significant move to increase its stake in the U.S. LNG market with its acquisition of Tellurian and Driftwood LNG.

Glencore Stock Is Highly Undervalued

The Global Investor | Strong Buy

There is something about Glencore plc (OTCPK:GLNCY)(OTCPK:GLCNF) for me. Maybe it is the history of its founder, Marc Rich. My finance professor more than 20 years ago, before Glencore was listed, told me you can't start a bank, but you can start a commodity trading operation—just look what Marc Rich achieved. Maybe it is because Glencore is the only major mining company with a significant commodity trading operation, which gives it profit opportunities in times of volatile markets.

As I think about it more, I think it's the culture within Glencore of always seeking value. The company didn't budge a few years ago when institutional investors started virtue signalling on coal, it stuck to its guns. That is now paying off. Today, Glencore is still seeking value. It stayed disciplined in the merger talks with Teck and in the end bought Teck's coal business EVR for $7bn. The company has exited agriculture and now holds a stake in Bunge Global (BG) which it will only divest at an opportune time. And as discussed on the 2024 earnings announcement, Glencore is looking at the options to move its primary listing from the London Stock Exchange to get a better valuation, likely on the New York Stock Exchange, or perhaps on the Australian Securities Exchange, which would be following in the footsteps of BHP (BHP).

Chief Executive Officer Gary Nagle has said that M&A is in Glencore's DNA. While it is always looking for opportunities to buy and sell assets, "Everything is for sale at the right price," he said in the analyst Q&A. Nagle said the best investment right now is the company's own shares, “We're very happy to be in the market buying back our own stock." Indeed, in the last three and a half years, the company has bought back nearly 10% of its stock and Glencore committed to another tranche of share buybacks with an aggregate value of up to USD 1 billion (subject to market conditions), with this program planned to be completed by 6 August 2025.

Candel Therapeutics: Entering Its Pivotal Year

Sean Daly | Buy

Back in December, Candel Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:CADL) reported big phase 3 results, followed by an equally big public offering.

It was precisely the kind of “one-two punch” that is guaranteed to suck the euphoria from those long-suffering investors hungry for good news, but it was perhaps not unexpected. Fortunately, the ticker has shouldered on during this long cold winter, climbing on three distinct developments: a phase 2a for pancreatic cancer that is imminent, an FDA submission by mid-year, and “technically” positive signs in the price action. CADL is entering its pivotal year.

Headquartered in Needham, Massachusetts, Candel Therapeutics focuses on biological immunotherapies that leverage anti-tumor and immune-stimulatory elements. The operative principle of this line of inquiry appears obvious enough --"teach" the body's immune system to clearly identify and eliminate malignant cells-- but many firms have stumbled in this “viral therapy” approach, all ultimately failing to harness the potential of viruses to directly impact cancer.

The company, which employs 42 people, has developed several distinct proprietary platforms: adenovirus platform, herpes simplex virus [HSV] platform, and enLIGHTEN Discovery. The company’s lead candidate is CAN-2409. This HSV-derived adenovirus is injected by needle into solid tumors, forcing the tumor cells to express an enzyme that converts antiviral agents into an antimetabolite as well as manifest those viral-associated proteins which will immediately trigger an immune response.

The Honest Company Is Overvalued But A Long-Term Buy

Harold Goldmeier | Buy

We have great faith in the long-term sales and profit growth of personal care companies like The Honest Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:HNST). For instance, we remain positive about Helen of Troy Ltd. (HELE) and The Procter & Gamble Company (PG), though the stock of the former is -52% and the stock of the more diversified business, P&G, is +9% over the past 12 months. Their market caps are in the billions of dollars. The shares of Honest are +102.8% this past year.

Honest is a niche player in the baby and personal care products markets. Its market cap is $606.7M. Following a 20% pullback since November, we propose the stock deserves a buy rating. Management seems focused, and the consumer markets for its products are growing. To paraphrase a media influencer, personal care is not an overnight process but a lifetime obsession.

Honest stock attracted attention and momentum after its second-quarter earnings set a record for quarterly revenue. Marketing Honest brand baby products and wipes drove a higher gross margin. Honest posted an unexpectedly strong Q3 ’24 earnings report that beat all estimates, sparking a run-up in the share price since last December. The next earnings report is scheduled for March 5, 2025.

We believe Honest’s revenue and earnings are bound to grow; the baby care and personal care industries are vibrant. The consensus among analysts is that we expect Honest to generate a profit in 2025.

Hammond Power Solutions: Fear Over Trump Tariffs Causes An Opportunity

Fernanda Galvez Jalil | Buy

Hammond Power Solutions Inc. (HPS.A:CA)(OTCPK:HMDPF) is a Canadian company that provides electrical transformers that are critical products for growing industries, which provides a growth context for the coming years.

In the last 6 months, the stock has experienced a 40% drawdown due to the announcements about Trump's tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico. I think this will end up being just political noise rather than a new reality and at the current P/E of 15 Hammond would be a good opportunity.

Hammond designs transformers and other electrical equipment which allow the voltage of electricity to be changed to make it suitable for different applications. For example, the electricity that reaches our homes through power grids has a very high voltage, but the devices we use (such as televisions, computers, etc.) need a much lower, safer voltage to operate. Hammond transformers convert that high voltage electricity into lower, safer voltage that can be used.

Hammond has a somewhat peculiar customer distribution, since Mexico and the U.S. represent 60% of sales, Canada 35%, making North America the largest market and 95% of sales, but then there is 5% in India, and it is not a negligible market due to the size of the country, which is still in electrical development and because during Q3 2024 this segment grew 15% compared to Mexico and the U.S., which remained flat.

Innovative Food Holdings: Niche Player Facing Profitability Challenges

Douglas McKenny | Sell

Speciality food distribution company Innovative Food Holdings, Inc. (OTCQB:IVFH) operates a growing, but high-cost business in a very competitive industry. The firm has a history of losses and sustainable profits are unlikely to arrive in the near-term. Nevertheless, the market has placed a $114.34 million premium to its intrinsic value. In other words, virtually all the company’s market cap represents the market’s expectations for future growth. This highly risky loss maker is a dangerous stock to own.

Founded in June 1979 in Colorado as Alpha Solarco, IVFH existed till February 2003 as an unfocused conglomerate involved in different activities. In February 2003, the firm changed its name to Fiber Application Systems Technology, Ltd., before changing its name and merging to form Innovative Food Holdings, Inc. in Florida.

IVFH enables scalable businesses by providing them with specialty foods that are challenging to get through traditional channels. It does this by developing close relationships with the supply-and-distribution-side of the equation, from across the world, and then selecting what they believe are the best suppliers from a quality, uniqueness and reliability perspective, and supplying those specialty foods to professional chefs, restaurants, and other food service businesses, within twenty-four to seventy-two hours of an order through its logistics network. IVFH is skilled at assessing and certifying the food safety standards and supply chain capabilities of small batch producers, who usually do not sell through broad-based sales channels.

Blaize Holdings: Why I Am Betting On This AI Hardware And Software Small Cap

Geneva Investor | Strong Buy

At a recent AI summit in Paris, the CEO of Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL), Sundar Pichai, mentioned how he thinks “AI will be the most profound shift of our lifetimes”. Sam Altman, OpenAI’s founder, spoke about how he believes “In a decade perhaps everyone on earth will be capable of accomplishing more than the most impactful person can today.”

I can only agree with these observations, as I believe that AI has the potential to disrupt our economic and social fabric to an extent that has not been seen since the industrial revolution.

While I have observed many people thinking AI’s final “shape” is in the form of LLM chatbots such as ChatGPT, I think we have yet to see the most disruptive applications of this new technology in our society. This is why I have been researching the space extensively for the past few years and why I have been investing in Palantir Technologies, Inc. (PLTR) since 2023, covering it on Seeking Alpha since May 2024.

It is with that enthusiasm for AI in mind that, when Seeking Alpha launched its last analyst competition for “Best Small/Micro Cap Idea”, I set out to find new AI companies. That’s because there are no better sectors for finding an asymmetric bet in 2025 than AI, in my view.

After much research, I discuss my findings in this article: a company called Blaize Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:BZAI), which was founded in 2010, but only listed on the Nasdaq in January this year. At the time of writing, and following a share price decline of 60%+ since its listing, I believe BZAI represents an asymmetric bet on AI.

The Other Five Fit For Mention

Xiaomi: Charging Ahead In The Ecosystem Race With A Spinning Flywheel

Astrada Advisors | Buy

Xiaomi Corporation's (OTCPK:XIACF)(OTCPK:XIACY) robust ecosystem, rivaling Apple and Amazon, drives growth across smartphones, IoT, and EV segments, with a 40% upside potential at a 3x forward multiple. Premium smartphones like Xiaomi 15 Ultra and expanding IoT products are key growth drivers, enhancing brand equity and consumer stickiness. The successful launch of the SU7 EV and upcoming Yu7 model highlight Xiaomi's competitive edge in the EV market, reinforcing its ecosystem. Valuation is conservative; Xiaomi should be valued on ecosystem strength, with potential for significant upside if key product launches succeed.

Ladder Capital: High Margin Of Dividend Safety

On the Pulse | Buy

Ladder Capital's (LADR) robust loan performance and healthy dividend coverage suggest potential for dividend growth in 2025, making it a top choice for passive income investors. Despite market stress, LADR avoided a dividend cut and maintained strong payout metrics, unlike peers such as Blackstone Mortgage Trust. The Company's book value is growing, and its current discount to book value is unjustified given its strong financial performance and improving provisions. With a brightening credit provisioning situation, Ladder Capital remains a compelling long-term investment, and I anticipate a dividend raise in 2025.

Indie Semiconductor: Riding The Wave Of Advanced Automotives

Cappuccino Finance | Buy

indie Semiconductor, Inc. (INDI) specializes in automotive semiconductors and software for advanced driver assistance systems, positioning itself in a high-growth market. The company’s focus on innovation and strategic partnerships enhances its competitive edge and growth potential. Risks include market volatility and dependency on the automotive industry’s adoption of advanced technologies. Overall, indie Semiconductor is a promising investment due to its strong market position and growth prospects in the automotive semiconductor sector.

Coherent: Growth Supported By Accelerating AI-Infrastructure Investments

GS Investing | Buy

I recommended a buy rating for Coherent Corp. (NYSE:COHR) when I wrote about it in October 2023, as I expected its datacom segment to be a key growth driver, supported by demand for AI. The stock has done really well since my coverage, now up ~240% since then. Based on my current outlook and analysis, I reiterate a buy rating. I believe the growth outlook remains robust as the pace of AI infrastructure investments does not appear to be slowing down anytime soon. The telecom segment recovery, with new product ramps, adds upside potential, supported by increased AI workload traffic between data centers. On the contrary, it could very well continue to accelerate for the foreseeable future.

HP Q1 Earnings Preview: Sentiment Low, AI PCs A Potential Growth Catalyst

FTS Research | Buy

HP Inc. (HPQ) is positioned for a potential rebound in 2025 due to Windows 10's end-of-life and the rise of AI PCs. Despite recent underperformance compared to competitors, HP is undervalued, presenting a buying opportunity ahead of earnings. EPS estimates are pessimistic ahead of earnings, low expectations set the stage for potential upside surprise. AI PCs could drive future growth, but their impact remains uncertain; HP's shift in production to Thailand mitigates some geopolitical risks. Overall, HP is a buy with caveats, especially considering the expected uptick in PC replacements and potential AI-driven market changes.

data.title: Elopak: Cheaper And More Resilient Than Peer Group
data.description:
Elopak (OTCPK:ELPKF)(ISIN:NO0011002586) is still waiting to see the capacity increases in the US, as discussed in our previous coverage. But new production lines are up in India, doubling that geography's volumes, but also dragging on average unit economics for the business. Otherwise, sales are slightly down due to negative timing effects on machine deliveries in Europe, with a positive European mix effect, with the US seeing altogether more solid demand but a decline in EBITDA as the Arkansas facilities near completion. Valuation remains really compelling considering the tailwinds that will follow the completion of construction in the US and better end markets than the peer group.

Latest Earnings

The latest earnings are quite straightforward. In Europe, sales of filling machines for cartons lagged, while the sales of cartons produced directly by Elopak (and supplied closure resales) performed well. This meant that European sales were down 6%, but EBITDA grew quite nicely by around 10% due to major mix benefits as equipment sales are significantly lower margin. The decline in sales for machines was primarily driven by timing effects, explaining why the decline was more dramatic than what could be expected just from CAPEX cycles lengthening and general investment reticence on the part of carton manufacturers. The improvements in EBITDA are quite remarkable though, since there were other negative mix effects coming from India, included in the EMEA segment with Europe, that put pressure on these EMEA segment results due to lower selling prices in India. India's contribution rose because the production line in there has been completed, and productive capacity has virtually doubled in that geography. Service revenues, also typically higher margin also saw growth in EMEA.

In the North American segment, the overall sales picture was quite solid. Sales were up 4.7%, though there was a lower level of sales in filling machines here as well. Carton volumes carried the results. Ordinarily this may have resulted in a positive mix effect, but EBITDA declined by 3% due to the costs associated with the investments being made in Arkansas, that is on track to increase NA production by around 66%, and overall Elopak sales by around 20-25% as clarified in our previous coverage.

Overall, sales declined 1.3%, and EBITDA was up a little less than 2% thanks to the overall mix effect benefits offsetting investment-related costs in the US.

Bottom Line

Elopak has traded down around 10% since our last coverage as of OSE data. The case remains highly compelling. Firstly, the 20-25% increase in total revenues expected after the Arkansas facility's completion in the coming H1 is going to meaningfully scale the business. This will scale the carton business specifically, which is also higher margin and has driven this quarter's mix effects. The completion of the facility, while introducing new depreciation costs, will also mean the end of other frictional costs being incurred in the project's construction. Finally, in terms of valuation, the company remains very attractive. Being similarly well-placed vis-à-vis the shift in packaging away from plastic, it trades at a significant discount to paper and packaging peers like International Paper (IP). Peer average multiples are around 10.3x EV/EBITDA. Elopak is at below 8x. Elopak also offers a more specialized product together with the entire carton filling solution to go with it. Their offering is more differentiated than a paper and packaging major, and it also is more focused on exposure just to consumer food products, namely dairy, whereas packaging majors will have unattractive exposures in specialty paper (with perhaps luxury market exposure), communication paper and construction paper that are all more at risk in the current uncertain macroeconomic environment. Mondi (OTCPK:MONDY) trades at a similar multiple, but has more unattractive exposure, like office (also in secular decline) and construction paper. It has more imminent growth drivers, better end markets and is cheaper than its less obscure peers.
